diagrams.title: Dynamic Array Amortized Growth
diagrams.description: Shows a dynamic array growing from size 1 to 8 through doubling operations, with cost annotations at each stage. The diagram illustrates how expensive resize operations are offset by many cheap insertions, demonstrating the amortized O(1) cost per operation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how array doubling amortizes the cost of expensive resize operations, showing that n insertions cost O(n) total, making amortized cost O(1) per insertion.

diagrams.title: Binary Counter Increment Sequence
diagrams.description: Displays a 4-bit binary counter incrementing from 0000 to 0111, showing which bits flip at each step. Highlights how most increments only flip one bit (cheap), while occasionally many bits flip (expensive), but overall amortized cost remains O(1).
elements: flip indicators (arrows showing which bits change)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates amortized analysis using a binary counter, showing how the expensive bit flips are compensated by many cheap flips, resulting in O(1) amortized cost per increment.

diagrams.title: Stack Operations Cost Analysis
diagrams.description: Illustrates a sequence of stack operations (push, pop) with their actual costs and amortized costs. Shows a scenario with Multipop operations that remove multiple elements, demonstrating how expensive operations are amortized over the sequence.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how Multipop and similar operations work in amortized analysis, showing how we can assign potential (credits) to elements to prove O(1) amortized cost per operation in stack operations.
